text.title: Idempotent Message Queue Consumers Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real mq-idempotent-consumers deployments. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer dedupes by broker delivery_tag / MessageId; tags change on retry; same business event processed twice.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused broker envelope IDs with payload-stable IDs.
antipattern.fix: Embed idempotency_key in the payload; dedup by that key, not the envelope ID.

antipattern.symptom: Two workers receive the same message simultaneously; both see 'not seen'; both execute the side-effect; deduplication never fires.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote the dedup marker AFTER the side-effect, missing the race window.
antipattern.fix: Reserve-before-execute: atomic SETNX before any side-effect; release on commit; cleanup on failure.

antipattern.symptom: Consumer acks the broker before the DB commit; commit fails; message is gone; the business event is silently lost.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated ack as 'received' rather than 'processed'.
antipattern.fix: Always ack AFTER the DB commit completes. On commit failure nack with requeue.

antipattern.symptom: Producer publishes the event before committing the DB transaction; consumer sees an order that does not exist in the DB.
antipattern.root-cause: Author tried to make publish + DB atomic by ordering inside the transaction.
antipattern.fix: Transactional outbox: write the message to an outbox table inside the transaction; a relay publishes from the outbox after commit.
